goal: Add GoalCompletionAuditReceipt/v1 as a deterministic pre-closeout receipt that compares a goal objective hash, requirement matrix, evidence refs, blockers, and a final verdict before any goal-ready or done claim can be supported.

forbiddenFiles: raw goal transcripts, prompts, secrets, or token values

nonGoals: Do not mark the active goal complete.
Do not replace Judge/PM audit; this receipt is a prerequisite evidence packet and verifier input.
Do not infer completion from green CI, PR creation, local tests, runtime cards, or reviewer mailbox text alone.
Do not mutate Codex source or external trackers in this slice.
Do not add a public closeout command unless a later production wiring slice intentionally owns it.
implementationApproach: Define GoalCompletionAuditReceipt/v1 under src/lib/delivery-truth as a private, fixture-backed receipt contract with validation split into a named deep module.

Store objective identity as repo-relative objectiveSourcePath, objectivePointer, objectiveSourceHeadSha, objectiveSourceSha256, objectiveHash, hashAlgorithm=sha256, and canonicalizationVersion rather than raw objective text so the receipt can compare goal identity without copying full prompts or transcripts.

Canonicalize objective text by reading UTF-8 bytes from the resolved source artifact, converting CRLF/CR to LF, preserving all other bytes, and hashing sha256 over the canonical bytes with a sha256:<hex> prefix.

Fail closed when the objective source path cannot be resolved under the evaluated repository head, when objectiveSourceHeadSha does not match the evaluated head SHA or an explicit immutable source digest, or when the recorded objectiveSourceSha256/objectiveHash do not match the source bytes.
Model a requirement matrix where each requirement has id, description, required flag, status, freshness, evidenceRefs, blockerRefs, and optional verdictRef.

Model blockers with blocker class, stable blocker key, owner, evidence refs, next action, and derived consecutiveGoalTurns so one-turn blockers continue while repeated blockers can recommend blocked status only after the existing threshold.
Derive consecutiveGoalTurns from prior goal receipts/state by stable blocker key; do not accept caller-supplied consecutive counts as authoritative input.
Build a verdict that is readyForDoneClaim only when all required requirements pass with current freshness and no unresolved blockers.
Return blocked or unknown when evidence is missing, stale, partial, or when the receipt cannot prove objective/requirement coverage.

Publish JSON Schema, example, manifest entry, TypeScript builder/validator, a narrow script-backed validator for agent-operable JSON receipt checks, and fixtures for complete, partial, stale, one-turn blocker, repeated-blocker, objective canonicalization, and objective-source drift cases.
implementationInvariants: Blocked-status recommendation threshold is exactly three consecutive goal turns for the same stable blocker key, matching the active goal-governor policy.
Two consecutive goal turns with the same blocker key must keep goalStatusRecommendation=continue.
Three consecutive goal turns with the same blocker key may produce goalStatusRecommendation=blocked but must not mutate goal state.
Missing blocker history, non-monotonic blocker history, or unstable blocker keys must fail closed to unknown or blocked and cannot support done claims.
Agent-operable validation must be available through scripts/validate-goal-completion-audit-receipt.cjs against the schema/example fixture; this is a validator, not a public closeout command.
GoalCompletionAuditReceipt/v1 remains additive to delivery-truth and Judge/PM audit; it cannot weaken existing goal_ready_for_judge_pm requirements.
riskControls: The receipt must not accept raw prompt/transcript/secret fields.
The verdict must keep goal status recommendation separate from actually changing any goal state.
Stale or missing evidence must be orientation only and cannot support done claims.
A one-turn blocker must not recommend blocked; repeated blockers must encode the three-consecutive-turn threshold explicitly and derive recurrence from history.
Objective identity must be bound to the evaluated head SHA or an immutable source digest, not just a free-form objectiveRef string.
Requirement ids and evidence refs must be non-empty, unique where needed, and safe pointer-like strings.
rollback: Remove goal-completion-audit-receipt TypeScript module and tests.
Remove generated local review artifacts for PU-029 if they are not promoted.
